matches.match_rationale: Bank deposit slips, handwritten cash count sheets, government voucher PDFs, and payment gateway reconciliation files in non-machine-readable formats all need digitization before they can be matched. OCR and form extraction directly addresses the manual re-keying step where tellers transcribe deposit slips into the ledger. For a 500-staff multi-campus operator receiving PHP 150k cash daily per campus, this is the upstream bottleneck that cascades into month-end chaos.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A document ingestion pipeline that OCRs scanned deposit slips and handwritten cash summaries, extracting amount, date, reference, and campus — feeding structured records directly into the AR matching queue without manual keying.

matches.match_rationale: Payment gateway reconciliation files arrive in proprietary CSV/Excel formats with transaction IDs that don't map to student enrollment codes. Government subsidy batches arrive as lump sums referencing voucher numbers, not student IDs. Each source has partial structure but incompatible schemas. AI-assisted schema mapping and normalization can convert all inbound payment data into a canonical format aligned to the AR ledger's invoice schema — the exact transformation step the finance team currently does manually over 3-5 days.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An ETL layer that ingests gateway CSVs, bank MT940 statements, and government remittance advice files and normalizes them into a unified payment record schema with confidence scores on each field mapping — ready for matching against open invoices.

matches.match_rationale: The core matching problem is fuzzy: a student transfer reference says 'JUAN DELA CRUZ 2024' while the invoice is under enrollment code 'EDU-2024-04872'. Semantic/embedding-based matching can surface the most likely invoice match for each ambiguous payment reference far more accurately than string-exact lookup. This is the exact scenario — matching vague bank reference text to student invoice records — where semantic similarity outperforms rule-based lookup.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An invoice-matching engine that embeds both payment reference text and student invoice metadata (name, ID, amount, term), then ranks candidate matches by semantic similarity — presenting the top 3 matches to the teller for one-click confirmation instead of manual search.

matches.match_rationale: The PHP 2M vs PHP 1.95M month-end discrepancy is a symptom of undetected anomalies accumulating throughout the month: duplicate receipts, split deposits counted twice, cash drawer shortfalls, and lost transfers. Anomaly detection on the payment stream — flagging transactions that deviate from expected patterns (campus cash volume, student payment history, timing) — can surface these discrepancies in near-real-time rather than at month-end close.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A daily anomaly scoring run over all posted transactions that flags duplicate receipt candidates, unexpected campus-level cash volume deviations, and unmatched high-value transfers — giving the finance team a short exception list to investigate each morning rather than a month-end pile.

matches.match_rationale: The full reconciliation workflow — ingest bank statement, parse formats, fuzzy-match to invoices, flag exceptions, post confirmed matches to ledger, route unmatched items for human review — is a multi-step branching process that today requires 3-5 person-days of coordinated manual effort. A task automation agent can orchestrate the entire pipeline: pulling data from multiple sources, applying matching rules, escalating low-confidence matches, and writing confirmed postings back to the ERP. This is precisely the multi-step document-processing workflow the capability is designed for.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A daily reconciliation agent that pulls bank statements and gateway files, runs matching across all payment channels, auto-posts high-confidence matches, and produces a prioritized exception queue for human review — reducing month-end close from 3-5 days to same-day with an exception review session.
